title: Minsait frontend for the BBVA Foundation
description: Advanced development for BBVA's OpenWeb ecosystem, with Web Components, LitElement and integration with the Cells corporate framework.
body: For **Minsait**, I worked as a frontend engineer integrated in the project of the **BBVA Foundation** (fbbva.es), contributing to the development of the **OpenWeb** ecosystem, the group's corporate digital platform. ## Development with Web Components and LitElement The project required building modular and reusable interfaces with standard **Web Components** and the **LitElement** library, ensuring full compatibility with BBVA's corporate framework: **Cells**. Each component had to be autonomous, testable and integrable in very diverse contexts. ## Integration with APIs and code quality He was responsible for consuming **REST APIs** and maintaining high quality standards, implementing unit tests with **Chai** and **Mocha**. The build process was managed with **Node.js** and **NPM**, integrating linting, testing and building in an automated flow. ## Technical challenges - Cross-browser compatibility and backward compatibility in a demanding corporate environment - Maintenance of visual and functional coherence between dozens of components - Collaboration with distributed teams following strict documentation and code review standards ## Technologies - LiteElement - Web Components - JavaScript (ES6+) - REST APIs - Chai/Mocha - Node.js/NPM - SCSS - HTML5 Project: [fbbva.es](https://www.fbbva.es/)
